Arist. lib . 8. •th . cap . 10. let we consider a king ( say the philosopher ) in his kingdom , as we will a father of many child in his family . Societas enim patris ad filio , Regni prae se fert effigiem : the society of a father towards his child , represent the true form of a kingdom . let any man compare the power royal to what he will , and ( accord to Aristotle opinion ) he shall find no one thing that do fit so well with it , or give it so much fullness , as the title of father , who day and night beat his brain , and employ all his whole study on that , which be most fit for his child regard moor the good which he may be able to do they , than any profit that he expect to receive from they . how many time while his child , be a bed and a sleep , do the father lie awake , cast , and devise with himself , how he may maintain they , and better they in their estate ? what a deal of care do he take to get his daughter a good husband , who be dear unto he , than himself ? great be his care ; but much more ought the care of a king to be in give to their kingdom , good , faithful , and diligent minister , to the end that they may do all possible diligence , ( as have be say heretofore ) fix always their eye on the common good , and direct likewise their own particular in the same way . that which i will say , ( to express it in plain term ) be this ; that they be not to subject the office to the commodity of the man , but to seek out man , that be fit and sufficient for they . when Saul be resolve to recommend unto David that duel , or single combat against the giant Goliath , the better to accommodate he , as also therein to honour he the more , he put his own raiment upon he , and a helment of brass upon his head , and a brigandine , and all the rest of his own complete armour ; a earnest exhortation to a true niniʋitish repentance . wherein be brief declare : 1. what true repentance be . 2. how a man shall perceive it wrought in he . 3. exhortation to a due examination of every one self . 4. a right penitential prayer . 5. the bar which hinder Christ work in the soul . 6. what true and live faith be . also two question resolve : 1. from whence war do spring . 2. by what mean it end . together with other considerable matter fit for the time . per christvm TESTOR , anti-christʋm aspernor . Allen in pope head alley . 1643. to all man dedicate : and publish by a lover of truth , that they may hearty try and examine themselves in this troublesome and miserable time ; so , that each one may begin to enter into himself , and there make a narrow search ; whether he be in Christ and Christ in he ; or whether he be unite to the world , the devil , and his own flesh and blood . whether light or darkness have dominion in he and over he , for his servant he be and shall be who he obey , whether of sin unto death , or righteousness unto life everlasting . Repent accord to the repentance of Niniveh . O belove man ! who so ere thou be , consider serious in thy heart and mind , thy present external and internal estate and condition ; have thou ever have any cause to repent ? do thou ever do ? or leave undo any thing unadvised ? do thou ever repent thereof ? serious examine thy self , and help thy self thus . quest . what be repentance ? Ans. repentance be a divine work in man , whereby God make know to he , that he have forsake God , and his holy will , in the inward ground of his soul , and have turn himself from the all-in-all enlightening light of grace , Christ Jesus ; and through his own mind and fleshly-will reject the government of the holy ghost ; and yet imagine himself by his accustom outward worship and assume name of religion , to be a right true christian , although he evident live in the love of the world , in fleshly lust , and in spiritual and corporal pride . for which subtlety and all other deceit of the crafty serpent , God work in man a hearty sorrow and repentance , for comply with god enemy ; so that a man by divine grace begin open to acknowledge such heavy and inward fall , and departure from God , and his holy will , bitter to lament the same , and in very earnest to hate his accustom internal idolatry commit with the world , flesh and blood , and with the proud and holy-seeming devil , and so to forsake , relinquish , and abandon the same : that when he be tempt either inward or outward ; he then by the live power of Christ within he , resist , fight , subdue , conquer and overcome , and thus he let the new penitential light appear without selfness or arrogancy , and shine forth to the glory of the most holy name of God , and the edify and amendment of his neighbour , in a right divine love , accord to the example of Christ , without any selfness , for by the divine repentance ( which be true godly sorrow ) all selfness will at last be total mortify and swallow up . from hence arise another question . 2. quest . how may a man know that such repentance wrought by God , be in he ?
